dialogue.speaker: jacob
dialogue.englishText: Gather yourselves together, that I may tell you what shall happen to you in days to come.

dialogue.speaker: jacob
dialogue.englishText: Assemble and listen, O sons of Jacob; listen to Israel your father.

dialogue.speaker: jacob
dialogue.englishText: Reuben, you are my firstborn, my might, and the firstfruits of my strength, preeminent in dignity and preeminent in power.

dialogue.speaker: jacob
dialogue.englishText: Unstable as water, you shall not excel, because you went up to your father's bed; then you defiled it — he went up to my couch!

dialogue.speaker: jacob
dialogue.englishText: Simeon and Levi are brothers; weapons of violence are their swords.

dialogue.speaker: jacob
dialogue.englishText: Let my soul come not into their council; O my glory, be not joined to their company. For in their anger they killed men, and in their willfulness they hamstrung oxen.

dialogue.speaker: jacob
dialogue.englishText: Cursed be their anger, for it is fierce, and their wrath, for it is cruel! I will divide them in Jacob and scatter them in Israel.

dialogue.speaker: jacob
dialogue.englishText: Judah, your brothers shall praise you; your hand shall be on the neck of your enemies; your father's sons shall bow down before you.

dialogue.speaker: jacob
dialogue.englishText: Judah is a lion's cub; from the prey, my son, you have gone up. He stooped down; he crouched as a lion and as a lioness; who dares rouse him?

dialogue.speaker: jacob
dialogue.englishText: The scepter shall not depart from Judah, nor the ruler's staff from between his feet, until Shiloh comes; and to him shall be the obedience of the peoples.

dialogue.speaker: jacob
dialogue.englishText: Binding his foal to the vine and his donkey's colt to the choice vine, he has washed his garments in wine and his vesture in the blood of grapes.

dialogue.speaker: jacob
dialogue.englishText: His eyes are darker than wine, and his teeth whiter than milk.

dialogue.speaker: jacob
dialogue.englishText: Zebulun shall dwell at the shore of the sea; he shall become a haven for ships, and his border shall be at Sidon.

dialogue.speaker: jacob
dialogue.englishText: Issachar is a strong donkey, crouching between the sheepfolds.

dialogue.speaker: jacob
dialogue.englishText: He saw that a resting place was good, and that the land was pleasant, so he bowed his shoulder to bear and became a servant at forced labor.

dialogue.speaker: jacob
dialogue.englishText: Dan shall judge his people as one of the tribes of Israel.

dialogue.speaker: jacob
dialogue.englishText: Dan shall be a serpent in the way, a viper by the path, that bites the horse's heels so that his rider falls backward.

dialogue.speaker: jacob
dialogue.englishText: I wait for your salvation, O LORD.

dialogue.speaker: jacob
dialogue.englishText: Gad — a raiding band shall raid him, but he shall raid at their heels.

dialogue.speaker: jacob
dialogue.englishText: Asher's food shall be rich, and he shall yield royal delicacies.

dialogue.speaker: jacob
dialogue.englishText: Naphtali is a doe let loose that bears beautiful fawns.

dialogue.speaker: jacob
dialogue.englishText: Joseph is a fruitful bough, a fruitful bough by a spring; his branches run over the wall.

dialogue.speaker: jacob
dialogue.englishText: The archers bitterly attacked him, shot at him, and harassed him severely.

dialogue.speaker: jacob
dialogue.englishText: Yet his bow remained unmoved; his arms were made agile by the hands of the Mighty One of Jacob — from there is the Shepherd, the Stone of Israel.

dialogue.speaker: jacob
dialogue.englishText: By the God of your father who will help you, by the Almighty who will bless you — with blessings of heaven above, blessings of the deep that crouches beneath, blessings of the breasts and of the womb.

dialogue.speaker: jacob
dialogue.englishText: The blessings of your father are mighty beyond the blessings of my parents, up to the bounties of the everlasting hills. May they be on the head of Joseph, and on the brow of him who was set apart from his brothers.

dialogue.speaker: jacob
dialogue.englishText: Benjamin is a ravenous wolf, in the morning devouring the prey and at evening dividing the spoil.

dialogue.speaker: jacob
dialogue.englishText: I am to be gathered to my people; bury me with my fathers in the cave that is in the field of Ephron the Hittite.

dialogue.speaker: jacob
dialogue.englishText: In the cave that is in the field at Machpelah, to the east of Mamre, in the land of Canaan, which Abraham bought with the field from Ephron the Hittite to possess as a burying place.

dialogue.speaker: jacob
dialogue.englishText: There they buried Abraham and Sarah his wife; there they buried Isaac and Rebekah his wife; and there I buried Leah.

dialogue.speaker: jacob
dialogue.englishText: The field and the cave that is in it were purchased from the Hittites.
